meaning: adj.潜在的,潜伏的,隐藏的n.隐约的指印
examples: These children have a huge reserve of latent talent.
Advertisements attempt to project a latent meaning behind an overt message.
For systems transforming solar to thermal energy, the thermal energy may be stored in matter as either latent heat or sensible heat.
True potential is latent in the rugged terrain that few are willing to traverse.
Consumer-focused businesses have the latent power to exploit what is now called Big Data.
